name: Lead-Lag Mean Reversion
description: Trades cross-currency pairs back toward their USD-implied fair value. When the actual cross rate deviates from the implied rate (derived from two USD-leg majors), enter against the deviation expecting mean reversion.
backtestNote: PF 0.06–0.15 over 180 days. The signal exists but spread costs overwhelm the small mean-reversion moves. Could work on a zero-commission exchange or with tighter spreads.

name: Range Fade
description: The opposite of a breakout strategy. Waits for price to break the Asian session range and then reverse back inside, entering the fade. Based on the observation that most intraday breakouts are false.
backtestNote: PF 0.68 over 180 days. 51.5% win rate (highest of any strategy tested) but poor risk:reward — when the fade fails and the breakout is real, losses are large. Needs better stop management.

name: Correlation Pairs
description: Trades the spread between two highly correlated instruments (AUD/USD and NZD/USD, ~95% correlation). When their price ratio diverges beyond 2 standard deviations, bet on convergence. Market-neutral hedged position.
backtestNote: PF 0.42 over 180 days, 2480 trades. The concept is sound but overtrades on M1 data — the z-score threshold fires too often. Needs longer lookback or higher entry threshold.
